report.hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target interactions.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disru

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. However, the low pass rate suggests that additional sequence constraints or structural guidance may be needed to improve yield.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely enabling off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
These mutations are expected to introduce charged or polar residues at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.
